company.dd:
We are Uber. The go-getters. The kind of people who are relentless about our mission to help people go anywhere and get anything and earn their way. Movement is what we power. It’s our lifeblood. It runs through our veins. It’s what gets us out of bed each morning. It pushes us to constantly reimagine how we can move better. For you. For all the places you want to go. For all the things you want to get. For all the ways you want to earn. Across the entire world. In real time. At the incredible speed of now.

The idea for Uber was born on a snowy night in Paris in 2008, and ever since then our DNA of reimagination and reinvention carries on. We’ve grown into a global platform powering flexible earnings and the movement of people and things in ever expanding ways. We’ve gone from connecting rides on 4 wheels to 2 wheels to 18-wheel freight deliveries. From takeout meals to daily essentials to prescription drugs to just about anything you need at any time and earning your way. From drivers with background checks to real-time verification, safety is a top priority every single day. At Uber, the pursuit of reimagination is never finished, never stops, and is always just beginning.

backgroundSummary: I interviewed for a senior-level engineering position, which focused heavily on algorithmic efficiency and optimization techniques.
generalNotes: The interview focused on moving from a brute-force understanding to a more optimized approach using binary search for array-based problems.

rounds.name: Initial Screening
rounds.roundDescription: This was an initial technical screening focused on problem-solving with arrays, specifically looking for optimized searching algorithms.

question.title: Find Local Minima
question.statement:
Given an array of integers, find a local minimum. A local minimum is an element that is smaller than its neighbors.  If there are multiple local minima, return any one of them. You can assume the array is not empty and that the first and last elements are considered local minima if they are smaller than their single neighbor.

###### Input Format:
An array of integers.

###### Output Format:
An integer representing a local minimum.

###### Example 1:
```
Input: [3, 1, 2, 3]
Output: 1
```

###### Example 2:
```
Input: [1, 3, 2, 1]
Output: 1
```

###### Constraints:
The input array contains at least one element.

questions.rawQuestion: Find all the local minima in an array and return their indices. A local minimum is defined as an element that is equal to or less than its left and right neighbors. Then, provide an efficient version to find any single local minimum using binary search.
questions.candidateAnswerSummary: I explained that to find a single local minimum efficiently, we can use a binary search approach. By checking the middle element and its neighbors, we can determine which direction to move to find a decreasing trend that must eventually result in a local minimum.

dl: 1536. Uber Interview Experience for Senior Software Engineer
summary:
In total, there were 1 round of interview evaluating various skills as mentioned in each round below:

👉 Round 1 - Initial Screening (60 minutes): This was an initial technical screening focused on problem-solving with arrays, specifically looking for optimized searching algorithms.
